code: sensible_bulk(t1, ...)  # Default S3 method sensible_bulk(   t1,   t2,   v1,   v2 = NULL,   z1,   z2,   rho = 1.225,   cp = 1005,   k = 0.41,   min_wind = 0.1,   warn_threshold = 600,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' sensible_bulk(   t1,   rho = 1.225,   cp = 1005,   k = 0.41,   min_wind = 0.1,   warn_threshold = 600,   ... )

title: Sensible heat flux by a simple bulk-transfer approach — sensible_bulk
text: t1 Air temperature lower measurement height. ... arguments passed methods. t2 Air temperature upper measurement height. v1 Wind speed lower measurement height. v2 Optional wind speed upper measurement height. missing, v1 used. z1 Lower measurement height m. z2 Upper measurement height m. rho Air density kg m-3. Default 1.225. cp Specific heat capacity air J kg-1 K-1. Default 1005. k von Karman constant. Default 0.41. min_wind Minimum wind speed resistance calculation. Values threshold return NA. warn_threshold Absolute flux threshold diagnostic warnings.

title: Sensible heat flux by a simple bulk-transfer approach — sensible_bulk
text: Sensible heat flux W m-2.

title: Sensible heat flux by a simple bulk-transfer approach — sensible_bulk
text: sign convention follows package energy-balance convention: H > 0 means sensible heat flux away surface. Therefore, t1 lower air temperature t2 upper air temperature, positive t1 - t2 produces positive sensible heat flux.

what: Sensible Heat using Monin-Obukhov length — sensible_monin
title: Sensible Heat using Monin-Obukhov length — sensible_monin
text: Calculates sensible heat flux using Monin-Obukhov length. Positive flux signifies flux away surface, negative values signify flux towards surface.

title: Sensible Heat using Monin-Obukhov length — sensible_monin
code: sensible_monin(...)  # Default S3 method sensible_monin(   t1,   t2,   z1 = 2,   z2 = 10,   v1,   v2,   elev,   cap = NULL,   surface_type = NULL,   obs_height = NULL,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' sensible_monin(weather_station, cap = NULL, ...)

title: Sensible Heat using Monin-Obukhov length — sensible_monin
text: ... Additional arguments. t1 Air temperature lower height °C. t2 Air temperature upper height °C. z1 Lower height measurement m. z2 Upper height measurement m (Use highest point measurement values less disturbed). v1 Windspeed lower height (e.g. height anemometer) m/s. v2 Windspeed upper height m/s. elev Elevation sea level m. cap maximum absolute value stability parameter \(s_1\). Default NULL. surface_type Type surface. Options: r surface_properties$surface_type obs_height Height obstacle meters (m). weather_station Object class weather_station

title: Sensible Heat using Monin-Obukhov length — sensible_monin
text: Sensible heat flux W/m².

title: Sensible Heat using Monin-Obukhov length — sensible_monin
text: sensible heat flux (\(Q_h\)) using Monin-Obukhov method calculated : $$Q_h = - \rho \cdot c_p \cdot \frac{k \cdot u_* \cdot z_2}{\phi_h} \cdot \frac{\Delta \theta}{\Delta z}$$ : \(\rho\) air density, \(c_p\) specific heat capacity air, \(k\) von Kármán constant, \(u_*\) friction velocity, \(\phi_h\) stability correction function heat, \(\Delta \theta\) potential temperature gradient, \(\Delta z\) height difference measurements. stability correction function heat (\(\phi_h\)) calculated using gradient Richardson number (\(Ri_g\)) stability parameter (\(s_1\)). stability parameter ratio higher measurement height Monin-Obukhov length. Monin-Obukhov length values close zero, ratio can result high values, stability parameter (\(s_1\)) capped. default cap set NULL.

title: Sensible Heat using Monin-Obukhov length — sensible_monin
text: Bendix 2004, p. 77, eq. 4.6, Foken 2016, p. 362: Businger

title: Sensible Heat using Monin-Obukhov length — sensible_monin
code: # Calculate sensible heat flux using the Monin-Obukhov method sensible_monin(t1 = 20, t2 = 15, z1 = 2, z2 = 10, v1 = 3, v2 = 5, elev = 100, surface_type = "lawn") #> Warning: There are values above 600 W/m^2! #> [1] 27759.3

title: Sensible Heat Priestley-Taylor Method — sensible_priestley_taylor
text: ... Additional arguments. temp Air temperature °C. rad_bal Radiation balance W/m². soil_flux Soil flux W/m². surface_type Surface type, Priestley-Taylor coefficient selected. Options: surface_type options weather_station weather_station object.

title: Sensible Heat Priestley-Taylor Method — sensible_priestley_taylor
text: Sensible heat flux W/m².

title: Sensible Heat Priestley-Taylor Method — sensible_priestley_taylor
text: sensible heat flux (\(Q_h\)) using Priestley-Taylor method calculated : $$Q_h = \frac{(1 - \alpha) \cdot s + \gamma}{s + \gamma} \cdot (R_n - G)$$ : \(\alpha\) Priestley-Taylor coefficient specific surface type, \(s\) slope saturation vapor pressure curve, \(\gamma\) psychrometric constant, \(R_n\) net radiation, \(G\) soil heat flux.

title: Sensible Heat Priestley-Taylor Method — sensible_priestley_taylor
code: # Calculate sensible heat flux using the Priestley-Taylor method sensible_priestley_taylor(temp = 20, rad_bal = 200, soil_flux = 50, surface_type = "field") #> [1] 32.35636

title: Soil Attenuation Length — soil_attenuation

text: ... Additional arguments. texture Soil texture. Either "sand", "peat" "clay". moisture Soil moisture content cubic meters per cubic meter. soil_temp1 Temperature first depth °C. soil_temp2 Temperature second depth °C. soil_depth1 Depth first measurement m. soil_depth2 Depth second measurement m. weather_station weather_station object.

title: Soil Thermal Conductivity — soil_thermal_cond
code: soil_thermal_cond(...)  # Default S3 method soil_thermal_cond(texture, moisture, ...)  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' soil_thermal_cond(weather_station, ...)

title: Soil Thermal Conductivity — soil_thermal_cond
text: ... Additional arguments. texture Soil texture. Either "sand", "peat" "clay". moisture Soil moisture content cubic meters per cubic meter. weather_station weather_station object.

title: Soil Thermal Conductivity — soil_thermal_cond
text: Soil thermal conductivity W/m/K.

title: Soil Thermal Conductivity — soil_thermal_cond
text: thermal conductivity (\(\lambda\)) soil determined based texture moisture content. values interpolated measured data different soil types.

title: Soil Thermal Conductivity — soil_thermal_cond
text: Bendix 2004, p. 254.

title: Soil Thermal Conductivity — soil_thermal_cond
code: # Calculate soil thermal conductivity soil_thermal_cond(texture = "sand", moisture = 0.25) #> [1] 2.4

title: Solar azimuth — sol_azimuth
text: Calculates solar azimuth, compass direction sunlight coming specific point earth's surface.

title: Solar azimuth — sol_azimuth
code: sol_azimuth(...)  # Default S3 method sol_azimuth(datetime, lon, lat, ...)  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' sol_azimuth(weather_station, ...)

title: Solar azimuth — sol_azimuth
text: ... Named station fields, site parameters model assumptions. datetime POSIXlt POSIXct date-time vector. lon Longitude degrees. lat Latitude degrees. weather_station weather_station object.

title: Solar azimuth — sol_azimuth
text: Degree.

title: Solar azimuth — sol_azimuth
text: solar azimuth (\(\)) calculated : $$= \arccos\left(\frac{\sin(\delta) \cdot \cos(\phi) - \cos(\delta) \cdot \sin(\phi) \cdot \cos(H)}{\cos(h)}\right)$$ : \(\delta\) solar declination, \(\phi\) latitude, \(H\) hour angle, \(h\) solar elevation angle.

title: Solar azimuth — sol_azimuth
text: Bendix 2004, p. 243.

title: Solar azimuth — sol_azimuth
code: # Calculate solar azimuth sol_azimuth(as.POSIXlt("2022-06-21 12:00:00"), lon = 10, lat = 50) #> [1] 180.7879

title: Day angle — sol_day_angle
text: See year circle. first day (leap) year 0 degree last day 360 degrees.

title: Day angle — sol_day_angle
code: sol_day_angle(...)  # Default S3 method sol_day_angle(datetime, ...)  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' sol_day_angle(weather_station, ...)

title: Day angle — sol_day_angle
text: ... Named station fields, site parameters model assumptions. datetime POSIXlt POSIXct date-time vector. weather_station weather_station object.

title: Day angle — sol_day_angle
text: Degree.

title: Day angle — sol_day_angle
text: day angle (\(D\)) calculated : $$D = \frac{2\pi(J - 1)}{365}$$ : \(J\) Julian day.

title: Day angle — sol_day_angle
text: Bendix 2004, p. 243.

title: Day angle — sol_day_angle
code: # Calculate day angle sol_day_angle(as.POSIXlt("2022-06-21")) #> [1] 168.6575

title: Solar declination — sol_declination
text: Solar declination

title: Solar declination — sol_declination
code: sol_declination(...)  # Default S3 method sol_declination(datetime, ...)  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' sol_declination(weather_station, ...)

title: Solar declination — sol_declination
text: ... Named station fields, site parameters model assumptions. datetime POSIXlt POSIXct date-time vector. weather_station weather_station object.

title: Solar declination — sol_declination
text: Degree.

title: Solar declination — sol_declination
text: solar declination (\(\delta\)) angle rays sun plane Earth's equator. calculated : $$\delta = \arcsin(\sin(23.44^\circ) \cdot \sin(L))$$ : \(L\) ecliptic longitude.

title: Solar declination — sol_declination
text: Bendix 2004, p. 243.

title: Solar declination — sol_declination
code: # Calculate solar declination sol_declination(as.POSIXlt("2022-06-21")) #> [1] 23.43806

title: Eccentricity factor — sol_eccentricity
text: track Earth around Sun circle, like ellipse.

title: Eccentricity factor — sol_eccentricity
code: sol_eccentricity(...)  # Default S3 method sol_eccentricity(datetime, ...)  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' sol_eccentricity(weather_station, ...)

title: Eccentricity factor — sol_eccentricity
text: ... Named station fields, site parameters model assumptions. datetime POSIXlt POSIXct date-time vector. weather_station weather_station object.

title: Eccentricity factor — sol_eccentricity
text: Unitless.

title: Eccentricity factor — sol_eccentricity
text: eccentricity factor (\(E\)) accounts elliptical shape Earth's orbit around Sun. calculated : $$E = 1.00011 + 0.034221 \cdot \cos(D) + 0.00128 \cdot \sin(D) + 0.000719 \cdot \cos(2D) + 0.000719 \cdot \sin(2D)$$ : \(D\) day angle radians.

title: Eccentricity factor — sol_eccentricity
text: Bendix 2004, p. 243.

title: Eccentricity factor — sol_eccentricity
code: # Calculate eccentricity factor sol_eccentricity(as.POSIXlt("2022-06-21")) #> [1] 0.9671952

title: Solar ecliptic length — sol_ecliptic_length
text: Calculates solar ecliptic length, angle Earth's orbit around sun relative vernal equinox.

title: Solar ecliptic length — sol_ecliptic_length
code: sol_ecliptic_length(...)  # Default S3 method sol_ecliptic_length(datetime, ...)  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' sol_ecliptic_length(weather_station, ...)

title: Solar ecliptic length — sol_ecliptic_length
text: ... Named station fields, site parameters model assumptions. datetime POSIXlt POSIXct date-time vector. weather_station weather_station object.

title: Solar ecliptic length — sol_ecliptic_length
text: Degree.

title: Solar ecliptic length — sol_ecliptic_length
text: solar ecliptic length (\(L\)) calculated : $$L = 279.3 + 0.9856 \cdot J + 1.92 \cdot \sin(M)$$ : \(J\) Julian day, \(M\) solar medium anomaly radians.

title: Solar ecliptic length — sol_ecliptic_length
text: Bendix 2004, p. 243.

title: Solar ecliptic length — sol_ecliptic_length
code: # Calculate solar ecliptic length sol_ecliptic_length(as.POSIXlt("2022-06-21")) #> [1] 449.2837

title: Solar elevation angle — sol_elevation
text: Solar elevation angle

code: sol_elevation(...)  # Default S3 method sol_elevation(datetime, lon, lat, ...)  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' sol_elevation(weather_station, ...)

title: Solar elevation angle — sol_elevation
text: ... Named station fields, site parameters model assumptions. datetime POSIXlt POSIXct date-time vector. lon Longitude degrees. lat Latitude degrees. weather_station weather_station object.

title: Solar elevation angle — sol_elevation
text: Degree.

title: Solar elevation angle — sol_elevation
text: solar elevation angle (\(h\)) apparent angle sun horizon. calculated : $$h = \arcsin(\sin(\phi) \cdot \sin(\delta) + \cos(\phi) \cdot \cos(\delta) \cdot \cos(H))$$ : \(\phi\) latitude, \(\delta\) solar declination, \(H\) hour angle.

title: Solar elevation angle — sol_elevation
text: Bendix 2004, p. 243.

title: Solar elevation angle — sol_elevation
code: # Calculate solar elevation angle sol_elevation(as.POSIXlt("2022-06-21"), lon = 10, lat = 50) #> [1] -16.56115

title: Solar hour angle — sol_hour_angle
text: Calculates solar hour angle, measure time since solar noon degrees.

title: Solar hour angle — sol_hour_angle
code: sol_hour_angle(...)  # Default S3 method sol_hour_angle(datetime, lon, ...)  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' sol_hour_angle(weather_station, ...)

title: Solar hour angle — sol_hour_angle
text: ... Named station fields, site parameters model assumptions. datetime POSIXlt POSIXct date-time vector. lon Longitude degrees. weather_station weather_station object.

title: Solar hour angle — sol_hour_angle
text: Degree.

title: Solar hour angle — sol_hour_angle
text: solar hour angle (\(H\)) calculated : $$H = 15 \cdot (T_m + E_t - 12)$$ : \(T_m\) solar medium suntime, \(E_t\) solar time formula.

title: Solar hour angle — sol_hour_angle
text: Bendix 2004, p. 243.

title: Solar hour angle — sol_hour_angle
code: # Calculate solar hour angle sol_hour_angle(as.POSIXlt("2022-06-21 12:00:00"), lon = 10) #> [1] 0.3840178

text: ... Named station fields, site parameters model assumptions.

text: Unitless.

text: Julian day (\(J\)) day year, ranging 1 366.

title: Julian day — sol_julian_day
code: # Calculate Julian day sol_julian_day(as.POSIXlt("2022-06-21")) #> [1] 172

title: Solar medium anomaly — sol_medium_anomaly
text: Calculates solar medium anomaly, angular distance Earth perihelion.

title: Solar medium anomaly — sol_medium_anomaly
code: sol_medium_anomaly(...)  # Default S3 method sol_medium_anomaly(datetime, ...)  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' sol_medium_anomaly(weather_station, ...)

title: Solar medium anomaly — sol_medium_anomaly
text: ... Named station fields, site parameters model assumptions. datetime POSIXlt POSIXct date-time vector. weather_station weather_station object.

title: Solar medium anomaly — sol_medium_anomaly
text: Degree.

title: Solar medium anomaly — sol_medium_anomaly
text: solar medium anomaly (\(M\)) calculated : $$M = 356.6 + 0.9856 \cdot J$$ : \(J\) Julian day.

title: Solar medium anomaly — sol_medium_anomaly
text: Bendix 2004, p. 243.

title: Solar medium anomaly — sol_medium_anomaly
code: # Calculate solar medium anomaly sol_medium_anomaly(as.POSIXlt("2022-06-21")) #> [1] 526.1232

title: Solar medium suntime — sol_medium_suntime
text: Calculates solar medium suntime, mean solar time adjusted observer's longitude.

title: Solar medium suntime — sol_medium_suntime
code: sol_medium_suntime(...)  # Default S3 method sol_medium_suntime(datetime, lon, ...)  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' sol_medium_suntime(weather_station, ...)

title: Solar medium suntime — sol_medium_suntime
text: ... Named station fields, site parameters model assumptions. datetime POSIXlt POSIXct date-time vector. lon Longitude degrees. weather_station weather_station object.

title: Solar medium suntime — sol_medium_suntime
text: Hour.

title: Solar medium suntime — sol_medium_suntime
text: solar medium suntime (\(T_m\)) calculated : $$T_m = T_{local} + \frac{lon}{15}$$ : \(T_{local}\) local time zone, \(lon\) longitude observer.

title: Solar medium suntime — sol_medium_suntime
text: Bendix 2004, p. 243.

title: Solar medium suntime — sol_medium_suntime
code: # Calculate solar medium suntime sol_medium_suntime(as.POSIXlt("2022-06-21 12:00:00"), lon = 10) #> [1] 10.66667

title: Solar time formula — sol_time_formula
text: Calculates solar time formula, corrects solar medium suntime account Earth's elliptical orbit axial tilt.

title: Solar time formula — sol_time_formula
code: sol_time_formula(...)  # Default S3 method sol_time_formula(datetime, lon, ...)  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' sol_time_formula(weather_station, ...)

title: Solar time formula — sol_time_formula
text: ... Named station fields, site parameters model assumptions. datetime POSIXlt POSIXct date-time vector. lon Longitude degrees. weather_station weather_station object.

title: Solar time formula — sol_time_formula
text: Hour.

title: Solar time formula — sol_time_formula
text: solar time formula (\(E_t\)) calculated : $$E_t = 0.1644 \cdot \sin(2L) - 0.1277 \cdot \sin(M)$$ : \(L\) ecliptic longitude, \(M\) solar medium anomaly.

title: Solar time formula — sol_time_formula
text: Bendix 2004, p. 243.

title: Solar time formula — sol_time_formula
code: # Calculate solar time formula sol_time_formula(as.POSIXlt("2022-06-21 12:00:00"), lon = 10) #> [1] 0.02560119

title: Potential Temperature — temp_pot_temp
text: Calculates potential air temperature, temperature parcel air expanded compressed adiabatically standard pressure (usually 1000 hPa).

title: Potential Temperature — temp_pot_temp
code: temp_pot_temp(...)  # Default S3 method temp_pot_temp(t, elev, ...)  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' temp_pot_temp(weather_station, height = "lower", ...)

title: Potential Temperature — temp_pot_temp
text: ... Additional arguments. t Temperature °C. elev Elevation sea level m. weather_station weather_station object. height Height measurement, either "upper" "lower".

title: Potential Temperature — temp_pot_temp
text: Potential temperature °C.

title: Potential Temperature — temp_pot_temp
text: potential temperature (\(\theta\)) calculated using formula: $$\theta = T \left(\frac{p_0}{p}\right)^{R/c_p}$$ : \(T\) temperature Kelvin, \(p_0\) standard pressure (1000 hPa), \(p\) actual pressure, \(R\) specific gas constant dry air (287 J/(kg·K)), \(c_p\) specific heat constant pressure (1004 J/(kg·K)). argument elev elevation sea level m. converted internally pressure using pres_p(). 1013.25 hPa default pres_p() used pressure estimation, potential temperature uses 1000 hPa reference pressure.

title: Potential Temperature — temp_pot_temp
text: Bendix 2004, p. 261.

title: Potential Temperature — temp_pot_temp
code: # Calculate potential temperature at a given temperature and elevation temp_pot_temp(t = 20, elev = 500) #> [1] 23.80798

title: Sky View Factor — terr_sky_view
text: Calculates sky view factor, represents much sky can seen given point.

title: Sky View Factor — terr_sky_view
code: terr_sky_view(...)  # Default S3 method terr_sky_view(slope, valley, ...)  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' terr_sky_view(weather_station, ...)

title: Sky View Factor — terr_sky_view
text: ... Additional arguments passed methods. slope Slope terrain degrees. valley Logical value indicating location valley (TRUE) (FALSE). weather_station weather_station object.

title: Sky View Factor — terr_sky_view
text: Ratio 0 1, unitless, representing sky view factor.

title: Sky View Factor — terr_sky_view
text: sky view factor (\(SVF\)) calculated : $$SVF = \frac{1 + \cos(\theta)}{2}$$ non-valley locations, $$SVF = \cos(\theta)$$ valley locations, \(\theta\) slope angle. terrain view factor can calculated 1 - terr_sky_view(), represents much terrain can seen point.

title: Sky View Factor — terr_sky_view
code: # Sky view factor for a slope of 30 degrees not in a valley terr_sky_view(slope = 30, valley = FALSE) #> [1] 0.9330127  # Sky view factor for a slope of 30 degrees in a valley terr_sky_view(slope = 30, valley = TRUE) #> [1] 0.8660254

title: Terrain Angle — terr_terrain_angle
text: Calculates angle terrain slope incoming solar radiation.

title: Terrain Angle — terr_terrain_angle
code: terr_terrain_angle(...)  # Default S3 method terr_terrain_angle(datetime, lon, lat, slope, exposition, ...)  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' terr_terrain_angle(weather_station, ...)

title: Terrain Angle — terr_terrain_angle
text: ... Additional arguments passed methods. datetime POSIXlt POSIXct object representing date time. lon Longitude decimal degrees. lat Latitude decimal degrees. slope Slope terrain degrees. exposition Exposition slope degrees (direction slope faces). weather_station weather_station object.

title: Terrain Angle — terr_terrain_angle
text: Angle degrees terrain slope incoming solar radiation.

title: Terrain Angle — terr_terrain_angle
text: terrain angle (\(\theta_t\)) calculated : $$\theta_t = \arccos\left(\cos(\theta_s) \cdot \sin(\alpha) + \sin(\theta_s) \cdot \cos(\alpha) \cdot \cos(\phi - \beta)\right)$$ : \(\theta_s\) slope angle, \(\alpha\) solar elevation angle, \(\phi\) solar azimuth angle, \(\beta\) slope exposition angle.

title: Terrain Angle — terr_terrain_angle
text: Bendix 2004, p. 52 eq. 3.7.

title: Terrain Angle — terr_terrain_angle
code: # Calculate terrain angle for a given datetime, location, and slope datetime <- as.POSIXlt("2023-08-06 12:00:00", tz = "UTC") terr_terrain_angle(datetime, lon = 8.6841, lat = 50.1109, slope = 30, exposition = 90) #> [1] 44.80026

title: Transmittance due to aerosols — trans_aerosol
text: Calculates transmittance due fine particles air.

title: Transmittance due to aerosols — trans_aerosol
code: trans_aerosol(...)  # Default S3 method trans_aerosol(datetime, lon, lat, elev, temp, ..., vis = vis_default)  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' trans_aerosol(weather_station, ...)

title: Transmittance due to aerosols — trans_aerosol
text: ... Additional arguments. datetime POSIXlt object, date time observation. lon Longitude decimal degrees. lat Latitude decimal degrees. elev Elevation sea level meters. temp Air temperature °C. vis Visibility km, default vis_default. weather_station weather_station object.

title: Transmittance due to aerosols — trans_aerosol
text: Transmittance ratio 0 1, unitless.

title: Transmittance due to aerosols — trans_aerosol
text: transmittance due aerosols calculated using formula: $$T_{aerosol} = \exp(-x^{0.873} \cdot (1 + x - x^{0.7088}) \cdot M_{abs}^{0.9108})$$ \(x\) function visibility \(M_{abs}\) absolute optical air mass.

title: Transmittance due to aerosols — trans_aerosol
text: Bendix 2004, p. 246.

title: Transmittance due to aerosols — trans_aerosol
code: # Calculate transmittance due to aerosols trans_aerosol(datetime = as.POSIXlt("2023-08-06 12:00:00", tz = "UTC"), lon = 8.68, lat = 50.77, elev = 100, temp = 20, vis = 50) #> [1] 0.865632

title: Absolute optical air mass — trans_air_mass_abs
text: Calculates absolute optical air mass.

title: Absolute optical air mass — trans_air_mass_abs
code: trans_air_mass_abs(...)  # Default S3 method trans_air_mass_abs(datetime, lon, lat, elev, temp, ...)  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' trans_air_mass_abs(weather_station, ...)

title: Absolute optical air mass — trans_air_mass_abs
text: ... Additional arguments. datetime POSIXlt object, date time observation. lon Longitude decimal degrees. lat Latitude decimal degrees. elev Elevation sea level meters. temp Air temperature °C. weather_station weather_station object.

title: Absolute optical air mass — trans_air_mass_abs
text: Absolute optical air mass, unitless.

title: Absolute optical air mass — trans_air_mass_abs
text: absolute optical air mass calculated using formula: $$M_{abs} = M_{rel} \cdot \frac{p}{p_0}$$ \(M_{rel}\) relative optical air mass, \(p\) local air pressure, \(p_0\) standard pressure (1013.25 hPa).

title: Absolute optical air mass — trans_air_mass_abs
text: Bendix 2004, p. 247.

title: Absolute optical air mass — trans_air_mass_abs
code: # Calculate absolute optical air mass trans_air_mass_abs(datetime = as.POSIXlt("2023-08-06 12:00:00", tz = "UTC"), lon = 8.68, lat = 50.77, elev = 100, temp = 20) #> [1] 1.0834

title: Relative optical air mass — trans_air_mass_rel
text: Calculates relative optical air mass.

title: Relative optical air mass — trans_air_mass_rel
code: trans_air_mass_rel(...)  # Default S3 method trans_air_mass_rel(datetime, lon, lat, ...)  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' trans_air_mass_rel(weather_station, ...)

title: Relative optical air mass — trans_air_mass_rel
text: ... Additional arguments. datetime POSIXlt object, date time observation. lon Longitude decimal degrees. lat Latitude decimal degrees. weather_station weather_station object.

title: Relative optical air mass — trans_air_mass_rel
text: Relative optical air mass, unitless.

title: Relative optical air mass — trans_air_mass_rel
text: relative optical air mass calculated using formula: $$M_{rel} = \frac{1}{\sin(elevation) + 1.5 \cdot elevation^{-0.72}}$$ \(elevation\) solar elevation angle degrees.

title: Relative optical air mass — trans_air_mass_rel
text: Bendix 2004, p. 246.

title: Relative optical air mass — trans_air_mass_rel
code: # Calculate relative optical air mass trans_air_mass_rel(datetime = as.POSIXlt("2023-08-06 12:00:00", tz = "UTC"), lon = 8.68, lat = 50.77) #> [1] 1.096104

title: Transmittance due to gas — trans_gas
text: Calculates transmittance due O\(_2\) CO\(_2\).

title: Transmittance due to gas — trans_gas
code: trans_gas(...)  # Default S3 method trans_gas(datetime, lon, lat, elev, temp, ...)  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' trans_gas(weather_station, ...)

title: Transmittance due to gas — trans_gas
text: ... Additional arguments. datetime POSIXlt object, date time observation. lon Longitude decimal degrees. lat Latitude decimal degrees. elev Elevation sea level meters. temp Air temperature °C. weather_station weather_station object.

title: Transmittance due to gas — trans_gas
text: Transmittance ratio 0 1, unitless.

title: Transmittance due to gas — trans_gas
text: transmittance due gases calculated using formula: $$T_{gas} = \exp(-0.0127 \cdot M_{abs}^{0.26})$$ \(M_{abs}\) absolute optical air mass.

title: Transmittance due to gas — trans_gas
text: Bendix 2004, p. 246.

title: Transmittance due to gas — trans_gas
code: # Calculate transmittance due to gas trans_gas(datetime = as.POSIXlt("2023-08-06 12:00:00", tz = "UTC"), lon = 8.68, lat = 50.77, elev = 100, temp = 20) #> [1] 0.9871164

title: Transmittance due to ozone — trans_ozone
text: Calculates transmittance due atmospheric ozone.

title: Transmittance due to ozone — trans_ozone
code: trans_ozone(...)  # Default S3 method trans_ozone(datetime, lon, lat, ..., ozone_column = ozone_column_default)  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' trans_ozone(weather_station, ...)

title: Transmittance due to ozone — trans_ozone
text: ... Additional arguments. datetime POSIXlt object, date time observation. lon Longitude decimal degrees. lat Latitude decimal degrees. ozone_column Atmospheric ozone column cm, default ozone_column_default. weather_station weather_station object.

title: Transmittance due to ozone — trans_ozone
text: Transmittance ratio 0 1, unitless.

title: Transmittance due to ozone — trans_ozone
text: transmittance due ozone calculated using formula: $$T_{ozone} = 1 - (0.1611 \cdot x \cdot (1 + 139.48 \cdot x)^{-0.3035} - 0.002715 \cdot x \cdot (1 + 0.044 \cdot x + 0.0003 \cdot x^2)^{-1})$$ \(x\) product ozone column relative optical air mass.

title: Transmittance due to ozone — trans_ozone
text: Bendix 2004, p. 245.

title: Transmittance due to ozone — trans_ozone
code: # Calculate transmittance due to ozone trans_ozone(datetime = as.POSIXlt("2023-08-06 12:00:00", tz = "UTC"), lon = 8.68, lat = 50.77, ozone_column = 0.3) #> [1] 0.9843997

title: Displacement height — turb_displacement
text: Calculates displacement height caused obstacle (e.g., crop field). function works vegetation urban environments.

title: Displacement height — turb_displacement
code: turb_displacement(...)  # Default S3 method turb_displacement(obs_height, surroundings = "vegetation", ...)  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' turb_displacement(weather_station, surroundings = "vegetation", ...)

title: Displacement height — turb_displacement
text: ... Additional arguments. obs_height Numeric. Height vegetation buildings meters (m). surroundings Character. Type surroundings. Options: "vegetation" "city". weather_station Object class weather_station

title: Displacement height — turb_displacement
text: Numeric. Displacement height meters (m).

title: Displacement height — turb_displacement
text: function calculates displacement height (\(d\)) caused obstacle, vegetation buildings. displacement height important parameter boundary layer meteorology affects wind profile near ground. vegetation, displacement height calculated two-thirds obstacle height. urban environments (dense housing), displacement height calculated 80% obstacle height.

title: Displacement height — turb_displacement
text: Bendix, J. (2004). Weather Climate: Introduction. Springer.

title: Displacement height — turb_displacement
code: # Calculate displacement height for vegetation with a height of 10 meters turb_displacement(obs_height = 10, surroundings = "vegetation") #> [1] 6.666667  # Calculate displacement height for a city with buildings of height 10 meters turb_displacement(obs_height = 10, surroundings = "city") #> [1] 8

title: Gradient-Richardson-Number — turb_flux_grad_rich_no
text: Calculation Gradient-Richardson-Number. number represents stability atmosphere. Negative values signify unstable conditions, positive values signify stable conditions, whereas values around zero represent neutral conditions.

title: Gradient-Richardson-Number — turb_flux_grad_rich_no
code: turb_flux_grad_rich_no(...)  # Default S3 method turb_flux_grad_rich_no(t1, t2, z1 = 2, z2 = 10, v1, v2, elev, ...)  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' turb_flux_grad_rich_no(weather_station, ...)

text: ... title: Gradient-Richardson-Number — turb_flux_grad_rich_no
text: stability class string: "unstable", "neutral", "stable", NA, according current Gradient-Richardson-Number thresholds.

title: Gradient-Richardson-Number — turb_flux_grad_rich_no
text: Bendix 2004, p. 43, eq. 2.5

title: Roughness length — turb_roughness_length
text: Calculates roughness length surface based obstacle height type surface.

title: Roughness length — turb_roughness_length
code: turb_roughness_length(...)  # Default S3 method turb_roughness_length(surface_type = NULL, obs_height = NULL, ...)  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' turb_roughness_length(weather_station, ...)

title: Roughness length — turb_roughness_length
text: ... Additional arguments. surface_type Type surface. Options: r surface_properties$surface_type obs_height Height obstacle meters (m). weather_station Object class weather_station

title: Roughness length — turb_roughness_length
text: Numeric. Roughness length meters (m). Numeric. Roughness length meters (m).

title: Roughness length — turb_roughness_length
text: Possible surface types : "field", "acre", "lawn", "street", "agriculture", "settlement", "coniferous forest", "deciduous forest", "mixed forest", "city", "water", "shrub". need specify one surface_type obs_height. function calculates roughness length (\(z_0\)) surface. roughness length measure roughness surface, affects wind profile near ground. can calculated either based height obstacles surface specifying type surface. obstacle height (obs_height) provided, roughness length calculated 10% obstacle height. surface type (surface_type) provided, roughness length looked predefined values.

title: Roughness length — turb_roughness_length
text: Bendix 2004, p. 239

title: Roughness length — turb_roughness_length
code: # Calculates roughness length based on obstacle height turb_roughness_length(obs_height = 10) #> [1] 1  # Calculate roughness length based on surface type turb_roughness_length(surface_type = "deciduous forest") #> [1] 1.5

title: Friction velocity — turb_ustar
text: Calculates friction velocity surface.

title: Friction velocity — turb_ustar
code: turb_ustar(...)  # Default S3 method turb_ustar(v, z, surface_type = NULL, obs_height = NULL, ...)  # S3 method for class 'weather_station' turb_ustar(weather_station, obs_height = NULL, ...)

title: Friction velocity — turb_ustar
text: ... Additional arguments. v Numeric. Windspeed height anemometer meters per second (m/s). z Numeric. Height anemometer meters (m). surface_type Character. Type surface. Options: "field", "acre", "lawn", "street", "agriculture", "settlement", "coniferous forest", "deciduous forest", "mixed forest", "city", "water", "shrub". obs_height Numeric. Height obstacle meters (m). weather_station weather_station object.

title: Friction velocity — turb_ustar
text: Numeric. Friction velocity meters per second (m/s). Numeric. Friction velocity meters per second (m/s).

title: Friction velocity — turb_ustar
text: function calculates friction velocity (\(u_*\)) surface, measure shear stress exerted wind surface. friction velocity important boundary layer meteorology understanding momentum transfer. friction velocity calculated using formula: $$u_* = \frac{v \cdot 0.4}{\log(z / z_0)}$$ \(v\) windspeed height anemometer, \(z\) height anemometer, \(z_0\) roughness length. roughness length (\(z_0\)) can determined based obstacle height (obs_height) type surface (surface_type).

title: Friction velocity — turb_ustar
text: Bendix 2004, p. 239

title: Friction velocity — turb_ustar
code: # Calculate friction velocity based on obstacle height turb_ustar(v = 5, z = 10, obs_height = 1) #> [1] 0.4342945  # Calculate friction velocity based on surface type turb_ustar(v = 5, z = 10, surface_type = "lawn") #> [1] 0.5112444
